notes: AMD server/workstation CPU; first AMD64/x86-64 chip (Apr 2003); briefly dominant in 2-4 socket x86 servers 2004-2006 before Intel Core microarchitecture
AMD 64-bit extension of x86 (Opteron Apr 2003, Athlon 64 Sep 2003); Intel adopted as Intel 64 (EM64T) 2004; became dominant 64-bit desktop/server ISA
Intel 8086-derived ISA; 16-bit (1978), 32-bit (1985), 64-bit via AMD64 (2003) and Intel 64 (2004); dominant desktop/server ISA
HP/Intel joint 64-bit VLIW architecture; limited adoption confined to HP-UX/NonStop; killed by AMD64; last shipment 2021
